issues.description: Claim: 'College Board Unit Five, which is eight to eleven percent of the AP exam.' The reference text (lines 292-364) does NOT state any exam-weight percentage for Unit 5. Unsupported numeric claim.
Claim: 'You share ninety-nine point nine percent of your DNA with every other human on Earth.' The reference does NOT contain this statistic. Unsupported numeric claim.

Claim: 'Independent assortment alone gives roughly eight point four million unique chromosome combinations per gamete.' The reference DOES support '2^23 ≈ 8.4 million combinations in humans' (line 343-344). Supported.

Claim: 'two-N, forty-six chromosomes in humans' and 'N — twenty-three chromosomes.' The reference does not give the human chromosome number 46 or 23 explicitly (only 2^23 for assortment). Unsupported specific number, though 23 is implicit. Flagging the explicit '46.'

Claim: 'crossing over in Prophase One — non-sister chromatids of homologous pairs exchange DNA at chiasmata.' Reference supports crossing over at chiasmata in Prophase I between non-sister chromatids (lines 336-337, 342). Supported.
Claim: 'Mendel ran pea-plant crosses in the eighteen-sixties.' The reference contains NO date and no mention of pea plants or Mendel's experimental history. Unsupported date and historical detail.
Claim: '3:1 phenotype ratio and 1:2:1 genotype ratio' for monohybrid, and '9:3:3:1' for unlinked dihybrid. Reference supports both (lines 306-308). Supported.

Claim: 'red snapdragon crossed with white gives pink' and 'one red, two pink, one white' (1:2:1). Reference supports the snapdragon example and 1:2:1 ratio (lines 314-316). Supported. ABO codominance with A and B antigens — supported (lines 317-318).

AB × O cross worked example. Reference establishes ABO alleles (I^A, I^B, i) and codominance (lines 317-320), but does not work through this specific cross. Mechanism is consistent with reference; specific ratio (50% A, 50% B) is derived, not cited. Acceptable — not a hallucination, derivable from cited rules.

Claim: 'three alleles — I-A, I-B, and little-i — combining to make four blood types' and pleiotropy / sickle-cell affecting hemoglobin, anemia, organ damage, malaria resistance. Reference supports all of this (lines 319-322). Supported.

Claim: 'red-green color blindness, hemophilia, and Duchenne muscular dystrophy hit males far more often' and hemizygosity in XY males. Reference supports all three examples and hemizygosity (lines 327-329). Supported. Polygenic = height and skin color: supported (line 323-324).
Claim: 'Trisomy Twenty-One is Down syndrome, X-X-Y is Klinefelter, X-O is Turner.' Reference supports all three (lines 358-360). Supported. NOT flagged.

Claims: 'Read OpenStax Biology, Chapters Twelve and Thirteen' and 'Khan Academy AP Biology Unit Five.' Reference contains NO mention of OpenStax, chapter numbers, or Khan Academy. Unsupported source recommendations (specific chapter numbers and external resource names not in the reference).

summary: Traced ~30 specific claims across 16 sections. Supported by reference: ~20 claims (Mendel's three laws, 3:1 / 1:2:1 / 9:3:3:1 ratios, snapdragon example, ABO codominance + multiple alleles, sickle-cell pleiotropy, hemizygosity + 3 X-linked disease examples, polygenic examples, 2^23 ≈ 8.4 million, crossing over at chiasmata in Prophase I, Meiosis I reductional / II equational, nondisjunction → aneuploidy with Down/Klinefelter/Turner). Flagged as unsupported by THIS reference: (1) Unit 5 exam weight '8-11%', (2) '99.9% DNA shared between humans', (3) Mendel date '1860s' and 'pea plants' as historical detail, (4) human chromosome count '46', (5) OpenStax chapters 12-13 and Khan Academy citations in section 16. Verdict: critical because multiple specific numeric/historical/source claims have no support in the provided reference window, even though most are general biology common knowledge.
